nodes.blurb: Rock Gnome Artificer (Artillerist) from Halruaa. Stole a flying ship to see the world.
nodes.name: Fiz

nodes.blurb: Variant Human Paladin, Oath of Vengeance. Ex-militia of the Silver Marches.
nodes.name: Hal

nodes.blurb: Lightfoot Halfling Storm Sorcerer. Captain of the lost True Hand; adopted brother to Eno.
nodes.name: Toz

nodes.blurb: Half-Elf Nature Cleric of Eldath. Raised in the wilds; adopted by the Greenbottles.
nodes.name: Eno

nodes.blurb: Tiefling woman of Nightstone who openly swore an oath to Asmodeus.
nodes.name: Aganor

nodes.blurb: A figure in Silverymoon — per Zephyros, Baros can fashion a silver eagle animal messenger that the crew can use to contact the cloud giant from a distance.
nodes.name: Baros Ravenkind

nodes.blurb: Boss of the goblin cave in Ardeep Forest, wearing a horned black hat and holding a captive Nightstone woman under one hand and a javelin in the other.
nodes.name: Boz Hark

nodes.blurb: A halfling ship-seller who works the Waterdeep docks.
nodes.name: Brindle Stormtide

nodes.blurb: A Waterdeep contact of Naxene's and an enthusiastic dragon collector — he keeps a small silver dragon, which is illegal, and seems entirely unbothered by that fact.
nodes.name: Chazlauth

nodes.blurb: Claimant who took control of Nightstone after Lady Nandor's death in the cloud giant attack.
nodes.name: Corvin

nodes.blurb: An Ardeep elf druid encountered casting a ritual with floating stones inside the goblin cave near Nightstone.
nodes.name: Daniel Windraven

nodes.blurb: Lady-in-waiting of Nightstone.
nodes.name: Daphne

nodes.blurb: Leader of the village of Triboar, north up the road past Waterdeep.
nodes.name: Derothra Shendrel

nodes.blurb: Deck hand of the True Hand, one of four survivors of the wreck off Nightstone.
nodes.name: Fish Hook Finn

nodes.blurb: Built a two-story walking mechanical statue in Waterdeep.
nodes.name: Garley Gond

nodes.blurb: Sailor of the True Hand.
nodes.name: Garret "Ox" Dorn

nodes.blurb: An unnamed goliath the crew met being turned away at Waterdeep's south gate, traveling with his wife.
nodes.name: Goliath of Cormeer

nodes.blurb: A frost giant said to be the crew's gateway to the Oracle in the Spine of the World.
nodes.name: Harshnag

nodes.blurb: A blonde Zhentarim agent, hunted across the Sword Coast — Xolkin and his flying-snake company were riding on her trail when they first turned up at Nightstone, believing she had gone to ground…
nodes.name: Kella

nodes.blurb: The lady of Nightstone, killed when a cloud giant's stone crushed her during the raid that took the village's namesake nightstone.
nodes.name: Lady Nandor

nodes.blurb: The Open Lord of Waterdeep — its ruler.
nodes.name: Laeral Silverhand

nodes.blurb: Cook of the True Hand, from the Pearl Isles.
nodes.name: Liera

nodes.blurb: An awakened tree in the grove outside Golden Fields, woken decades ago by a traveling druid.
nodes.name: Lifferloss

nodes.blurb: A Nightstone guard who briefed the crew on the situation after Corvin took over the village.
nodes.name: Merrick

nodes.blurb: A yeti who tends the bar at the Golden Fields inn.
nodes.name: Miros

nodes.blurb: Innkeeper of Nightstone and the local gossip hub — Molak knows what is stirring in the area better than anyone.
nodes.name: Molak

nodes.blurb: A noble of Waterdeep first met at the Golden Fields inn — stand-offish at first, ink-stained fingers, deep in her own notes on widespread giant activity.
nodes.name: Naxene

nodes.blurb: Navigator of the wrecked True Hand and one of the four survivors of the wreck near Nightstone.
nodes.name: Ol' Rourke

nodes.blurb: An ancient green dragon dwelling in Kryptgarden Forest.
nodes.name: Old Gnawbone

nodes.blurb: A halfling bard in Golden Fields.
nodes.name: Orin

nodes.blurb: The queen of the hill giants.
nodes.name: Queen Guh

nodes.blurb: Small, twitchy goblin from the Nightstone cave, assigned by the boss (Boz Hark) to lead the crew to the stalactite trouble.
nodes.name: Snigbat

nodes.blurb: A half-orc captain of the Golden Fields guard.
nodes.name: Strog Thunderblade

nodes.blurb: Castelan of Golden Fields and overall boss of the guards.
nodes.name: Tarn

nodes.blurb: The ogre that led the raid on Nightstone from the goblin cave near Ardeep Forest.
nodes.name: Thock

nodes.blurb: A bard who hangs around the Harshnag statue in Waterdeep.
nodes.name: Vandal Lovelace

nodes.blurb: A copper dragon and old ally of Force Gray, said to fly up and down the High Road.
nodes.name: Vexalanthus

nodes.blurb: Bandit captain and Zhentarim agent (winged-snake tattoo, small flying-snake familiar always circling him).
nodes.name: Xolkin

nodes.blurb: A dwarf Harper said to live in the Trades Ward of Waterdeep.
nodes.name: Yackerty

nodes.blurb: A cloud giant who ferries the party in his flying castle, taking them to Golden Fields.
nodes.name: Zephyros

nodes.blurb: A forest near Nightstone, popular with nobles for hunting.
nodes.name: Ardeep Forest

nodes.blurb: The "City of Coin" — Toz's home and the seat of the Greenbottle family, a well-known mercantile clan with a fleet of sailing vessels.
nodes.name: Athkatla

nodes.blurb: A northern town under attack by frost giants.
nodes.name: Bryn Shandar

nodes.blurb: Where Kella's father — said to be famous — lives, along with her grandfather Teldor.
nodes.name: Darkar

nodes.blurb: The settlement out of which the Kella bounty originated.
nodes.name: Darkhope

nodes.blurb: A tavern on the Waterdeep docks.
nodes.name: Deep Water Inn

nodes.blurb: A site sacred to the giant gods.
nodes.name: Eye of Annam

nodes.blurb: A farming town about twice the size of Nightstone, anchored by an abbey to Shontea — a wooden statue of the plump, smiling goddess surrounded by wheat stands at its heart.
nodes.name: Golden Fields

nodes.blurb: The southern technocracy Fiz hails from.
nodes.name: Halruaa

nodes.blurb: Lair of the green dragon Old Gnawbone, who Chazlauth thinks may be willing to help kill giants.
nodes.name: Kryptgarden Forest

nodes.blurb: A small village in the North near Ardeep Forest.
nodes.name: Nightstone

nodes.blurb: Home of Liera, the True Hand's cook and one of the wreck's four survivors.
nodes.name: Pearl Isles

nodes.blurb: A northern region near the Evermoor.
nodes.name: Silver Marches

nodes.blurb: A city in the Silver Marches.
nodes.name: Silverymoon

nodes.blurb: A vast mountain range in the far North.
nodes.name: Spine of the World

nodes.blurb: Toz's first command — a Greenbottle-financed ship that met an untimely end just eleven months after her maiden voyage, wrecked off the coast near Nightstone.
nodes.name: The True Hand

nodes.blurb: The district of Waterdeep where Yackerty the dwarf Harper is said to live — the crew's likely route into the Harpers' secret teleportation portal network.
nodes.name: Trades Ward

nodes.blurb: A town in the North suffering attacks from fire giants who have been digging holes in the surrounding land.
nodes.name: Triboar

nodes.blurb: The City of Splendors.
nodes.name: Waterdeep

nodes.blurb: The most popular tavern in Waterdeep, per Chazlauth.
nodes.name: Yawning Portal

nodes.blurb: A magical bladder recovered from the belly of the failing walking mechanical statue in Waterdeep.
nodes.name: Alchemy jug

nodes.blurb: A tidy leather satchel taken off the kobold that accompanied the blue dragon and half-dragon rider at Goldenfields.
nodes.name: Bag of Holding

nodes.blurb: A sorcerer's focus taken from the half-dragon rider who fell at Goldenfields.
nodes.name: Bloodwell Vial

nodes.blurb: Two liters of blue-dragon blood, drained from the fresh corpse into a giant-sized jar.
nodes.name: Blue dragon blood

nodes.blurb: A non-magical ring bearing the sigil of a blue dragon, taken from the Bag of Holding of the kobold who fell at Goldenfields alongside a young blue dragon and a half-dragon rider.
nodes.name: Blue dragon sigil ring

nodes.blurb: A single intact fang pulled from the young blue dragon's jaw after the fight outside Goldenfields.
nodes.name: Blue dragon tooth

nodes.blurb: A set of blue-steel thieves' tools etched with Draconic numerals along each pick.
nodes.name: Blue-steel thieves' tools

nodes.blurb: A palm-sized bronze figurine of a griffin, gifted by Zephyros after Eno befriended his tower's flock.
nodes.name: Bronze griffin figurine

nodes.blurb: A +1 magical blade pulled from a sarcophagus beneath Nightstone, retrieved via the secret passage into the crypt.
nodes.name: Cursed crypt sword

nodes.blurb: A weeping dagger taken off the half-dragon rider at Goldenfields.
nodes.name: Dagger of Venom

nodes.blurb: A sheaf of tightly-written notes, all in Draconic.
nodes.name: Draconic notes

nodes.blurb: A small volume by Volotham Gedarm found among Zephyros's floating stacks.
nodes.name: Giant Runes of the Realms

nodes.blurb: A butcher's knife too large for a humanoid hand, looted from a hill giant corpse after the second raid on Golden Fields.
nodes.name: Giant-sized skinning knife

nodes.blurb: The scripture of Valkur, god of sailors, given to Toz by the priest at the Wave Hall in Waterdeep after Toz confessed his new fear of water and the malevolent presence he has been dreaming of.
nodes.name: Holy book of Valkur

nodes.blurb: A wooden holy symbol of Silvanus, the woodland god, inlaid with gold.
nodes.name: Holy symbol of Silvanus

nodes.blurb: Salvaged from the wreck of the True Hand on the crew's first steps ashore.
nodes.name: Ornate genie bottle

nodes.blurb: A living circlet of vine pressed onto Fiz by Daniel Windraven at the Nightstone cave mouth — a Watch-token of the Emerald Enclave, the druidic network watching the sudden movements of giants across…
nodes.name: Vine bracelet

nodes.blurb: The front-page issue of the Waterdeep Wazoo the crew signed at the south gate — headline "Giant Killers," with a not-entirely-flattering ink sketch of the four.
nodes.name: Waterdeep Wazoo — "Giant Killers" issue

nodes.blurb: A slim stick of chalk that drifts on its own across a laid-out map, scribbling lines and fading them again — mapping the wind.
nodes.name: Wind-chalk

nodes.blurb: A magical item pressed on the crew by Xolkin — Zhentarim bandit captain — as thanks after Corvin's fall at Nightstone.
nodes.name: Xolkin's gift

nodes.blurb: Path is Spine of the World, with the giant Harshnag as guide.
nodes.name: Reach the Oracle

nodes.blurb: Last known to be in Silverymoon.
nodes.name: Find Harshnag

nodes.blurb: The wrecked crew of the True Hand stagger inland to Nightstone, find it overrun by goblins and dire wolves, and end up defending the ruined village alongside a party of adventurers hunting a missing…
nodes.name: Session 2025-09-23

nodes.blurb: Nightstone's fall came clear — cloud giants dropped rocks from the sky and stole the black stone — and the crew followed the trail into Ardeep to a goblin cave, where they met a druid of the Emerald…
nodes.name: Session 2025-11-12

nodes.blurb: A tense truce with the goblin boss got the children out alive, the crew wisely refused to trade blows with a black ooze roosting in a stalactite, and Molak gave up the genie bottle along with a name…
nodes.name: Session 2025-12-07

nodes.blurb: Back at Nightstone with black banners flying, the crew met the new claimant Corvin, learned the wider giant troubles, and split — Toz and Fiz brokered an oath with a tiefling cultist while Hal and…
nodes.name: Session 2025-12-17

nodes.blurb: The crew finally toppled Corvin's takeover of Nightstone — only for Aganor's vengeance to summon Destiny, a cambion of Asmodeus, forcing a fighting retreat before a cloud giant named Zephyros…
nodes.name: Session 2026-01-13

nodes.blurb: Aboard Zephyros's flying tower bound for Goldenfields, the crew rifled through giant-sized bookshelves, won gifts of map, chalk, book, and bronze griffin, then fought off a gargoyle ambush riding in…
nodes.name: Session 2026-01-27

nodes.blurb: Roused by Zephyros's dire warning of giant gods and an imminent assault, the crew set the wizard's tower down outside Goldenfields and weathered a brutal battle against a young blue dragon, a…
nodes.name: Session 2026-02-10

nodes.blurb: Zephyros set the crew down at Golden Fields, where a yeti barkeep, an awakened tree, and a Waterdhavian noble pointed them toward dragons, the Spine of the World, and a giant named Harshnag.
nodes.name: Session 2026-03-08

nodes.blurb: A goblin-bugbear-ogre raid swept through Golden Fields at night, and the crew fought through two waves before the abbey bell rang to warn that giants were coming.
nodes.name: Session 2026-05-12

nodes.blurb: Golden Fields takes its second raid since the crew arrived, and an insight check reveals what the hill giants are actually after.
nodes.name: Session 2026-06-02

nodes.blurb: Making landfall in Waterdeep, the Crew found themselves front-page news, chased leads on Harshnag and the Oracle through a dragon-mad wizard and a bard by his statue, then broke a mind-controlled bar…
nodes.name: Session 2026-06-16
